title: BeautiCraft
description: A modpack of mods to beautify Minecraft and improve the soundscape.
body:
![preview picture](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d96e343a42fd660833fb04672116d3c4e80759a5.jpeg)


This modpack, BeautiCraft, focuses on making Minecraft more beautiful and sonorous without changing the basic gameplay experience. It is packed with client-side improvements:

**👀Visual enhancement:** A large collection of mods and resource packs (incl. Fresh Animations, Physics Mod, Continuity, 3D models) for improved particles, animations, UI and textures for a more appealing look.

**🎧Acoustic modifications:** With mods such as AmbientSounds or Reactive Music, the soundscape becomes more diverse and immersive.

**🛠Quality-of-Life:** Includes minor adjustments to improve the user experience, such as the display of hunger/satiety values (AppleSkin), information about viewed entity (WTHIT), a zoom function (Zoomify) and a quick quit feature (FastQuit).

You can find which mods and resource packs are used in the version tab in the corresponding version.

The four shaders listed below are pre-installed in the modpack, and the FPS results reflect performance on my personal system:

| Shader Pack                            | Average FPS         | 1% Low FPS           |
|----------------------------------------|:------------------:|---------------------:|
| Complementary Shaders  | 47.4 FPS         | 36.2 FPS             |
| BSL Shaders (default)                  | 65.7 FPS            | 45.4 FPS             |
| MakeUp - Ultra Fast                    | 122 FPS             | 64.7 FPS             |
| Vanilla                                | 432 FPS             | 144.8 FPS            |

<details>
<summary>complete fps test</summary>
Note: The FPS were slightly lower because the recording program and server were running on the same computer.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/-p9P7ysvtKM" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

camera movement was made with [Typewriter](https://modrinth.com/plugin/typewriter) plugin

</details>


Test system:
- GPU: NVIDIA RTX 3060
- CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 5600X
- RAM: 16 GB
- Resolution: 2560×1440 (1440p)
- Minecraft Render Distance: 12 chunks
- Modpack version 3.1.0

This was a simple FPS test on my Minecraft server using a smooth camera fly-through to compare the performance of different shaders. It's not a full benchmark (e.g., no mobs, redstone, or heavy gameplay), but it gives a general idea of how well each shader performs in this environment.
